jokes.text: Kids! Your mother's on the phone, and it's her first day on set, so let's all make sure we keep her spirits high, okay?
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to rally the family for a supportive phone call
jokes.punchline: The immediate chaos that follows as everyone panics about basic phone operation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the chaos to follow, showing Johnny's optimistic leadership against family dysfunction

jokes.text: Moira mentions shooting next to a baklava factory
jokes.setup: Moira talks about sweetness in the air on set
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's literally because of a baklava factory
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and specific detail that deflates Moira's dramatic moment

jokes.text: She-she's not my mother. - Who are those voices? - Uh, your children!
jokes.setup: Moira can't recognize her own children's voices
jokes.punchline: Johnny having to explain that the voices are literally her children
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character work - shows Moira's self-absorption and disconnect from family reality in just a few words.

jokes.text: Oh Alexis, do you have strep again? - That was David!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to identify her children's voices
jokes.punchline: She mistakes David for Alexis having strep throat
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Moira's disconnect, plus the absurdity of confusing David's voice for sick Alexis.

jokes.text: David! Oh, David, how's the store? Any new customers, or is it still friendless?
jokes.setup: Moira asking about David's business
jokes.punchline: Casually describing his store as 'friendless' in the most matter-of-fact way
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - brutal honesty delivered with maternal concern. 'Friendless' is such a specific, cutting word choice that only she would use.

jokes.text: Alexis points out 'She already hung up' when Johnny says goodbye
jokes.setup: Johnny continues talking affectionately to Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira had already hung up
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward family moment with good cringe factor

jokes.text: Alexis says 'Oh! Burn, Dad!' followed by David's 'Oh my God that's so embarrassing, you must feel ridiculous'
jokes.setup: Johnny's embarrassing phone moment
jokes.punchline: Both kids pile on with increasingly harsh commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling dynamic and escalating cruelty toward their father

jokes.text: Your natural deodorant isn't working.
jokes.setup: David approaching Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt assessment delivered in her signature deadpan style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery. Economy is excellent - maximum impact with minimal words, very character-specific.

jokes.text: Stevie reveals David's poor score on relationship quiz from M'Lady Magazine 1991
jokes.setup: Stevie expresses concern about David and Patrick
jokes.punchline: She found his terrible score on an ancient magazine quiz
jokes.explanation: Great setup with specific absurd details (1991 magazine) and David's vulnerability

jokes.text: David corrects 'it's Major Lady Magazine' with wounded dignity
jokes.setup: Stevie calls it 'M'Lady Magazine'
jokes.punchline: David defensively corrects the name of his trash magazine
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - defending something indefensible with complete seriousness

jokes.text: Stevie observes David took the quiz multiple times despite calling it garbage
jokes.setup: David claims the quiz is garbage
jokes.punchline: Evidence shows he took it repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Nice character observation about David's insecurity despite his protests

jokes.text: David's comeback: 'You're dating a vet who irons his polo shirts'
jokes.setup: Alexis mocks David's relationship as 'snooze-town'
jokes.punchline: David points out Ted's uptight habits
jokes.explanation: Specific, visual detail that perfectly captures Ted's character

jokes.text: Alexis counters with 'Ted and I got Electrical Storm. So.'
jokes.setup: David's attack on her boring relationship
jokes.punchline: Alexis smugly reveals their high quiz score
jokes.explanation: Alexis's smug delivery makes this simple line effective

jokes.text: David asks 'Did you plug your hairdryer in with wet hands again?'
jokes.setup: Alexis claims high relationship electricity
jokes.punchline: David references her history of literal electrical accidents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay connecting relationship 'electricity' to literal electrical accidents

jokes.text: Alexis defensively responds 'that happened twice David, let it go'
jokes.setup: David's hairdryer reference
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits it happened twice, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation - her defense makes the original joke worse

jokes.text: David's response to 'adventure dates': 'sound like something a marriage counselor would proscribe as a last resort'
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions their Friday adventure dates
jokes.punchline: David's cynical interpretation of romantic activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect David cynicism with sophisticated vocabulary ('proscribe') making it funnier

jokes.text: David's dismissive 'It's merchandising day, actually' when called out
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests he has boring work to do
jokes.punchline: David proudly defends his mundane retail task
jokes.explanation: David's pride in mundane business tasks is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You guys could be a 'Total Power Failure.' - Mm! Eat glass.
jokes.setup: Stevie continuing to mock David's quiz result
jokes.punchline: David's angry 'eat glass' response
jokes.explanation: 'Eat glass' is a wonderfully absurd and dramatic David response to being mocked.

jokes.text: Moira's dramatic entrance: 'Caw! Caw! My teamster brethren! Yes, it's me, if you can believe it!'
jokes.setup: Moira arrives on a crow-themed movie set
jokes.punchline: She immediately adopts crow mannerisms and speech
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - instantly committing to character work in ridiculous circumstances

jokes.text: Moira introduces herself as 'Doctor Clara Beatrice Mandrake' adding 'Middle name wasn't scripted, but I've done my homework'
jokes.setup: Meeting the director as her character
jokes.punchline: She's added unnecessary backstory to a low-budget crow movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work showing Moira's over-preparation for terrible material

jokes.text: Moira references director breaking her down 'Like Stan Kubrick did to Shelley'
jokes.setup: Director seems dismissive
jokes.punchline: Moira compares low-budget crow movie to The Shining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira delusion connecting her B-movie to cinema history

jokes.text: Moira calls the script 'a total dumpster fire, of course' delivered casually
jokes.setup: Director asks about script problems
jokes.punchline: Moira matter-of-factly calls it terrible while staying committed
jokes.explanation: Great delivery of harsh truth with casual acceptance

jokes.text: It's an apocalyptic fantasy about mutant crows. I think we all know what we're making here. - A timely allegory about prejudice.
jokes.setup: Moira describing the ridiculous premise
jokes.punchline: The director's pretentious interpretation of obvious schlock
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Moira's realism and the director's pretentious spin on obvious B-movie material.

jokes.text: Moira's response: 'Caroline Kennedy once called it the Atlantic City of French Polynesia'
jokes.setup: Director mentions Bora Bora vacation plans
jokes.punchline: Moira delivers obscure, probably fake celebrity insult
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - absurd specificity and name-dropping that's probably fabricated

jokes.text: Roland's fears escalate to 'I go home and he's playing strip backgammon with my wife'
jokes.setup: Roland complaining about Johnny doing his job
jokes.punchline: Paranoid leap to sexual infidelity involving board games
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with specific weird detail (strip backgammon) that's very Roland

jokes.text: Roland continues: 'I look in the mirror and it's Johnny's face staring back at me?'
jokes.setup: Roland's paranoia about Johnny taking over
jokes.punchline: Escalates to existential identity crisis
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Roland's paranoia to philosophical level

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm the gutter guy, Johnny. Everybody knows I'm the gutter guy!
jokes.setup: Johnny doing Roland's maintenance work
jokes.punchline: Roland's desperate assertion of his professional identity as 'the gutter guy'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Roland's need for professional identity, even if it's just cleaning gutters.

jokes.text: Roland continues paranoid spiral: 'I go to my dermatologist, and Johnny's there getting my moles checked?'
jokes.setup: Roland's fear of Johnny taking over his life
jokes.punchline: Specific medical procedure invasion
jokes.explanation: Absurdly specific paranoid escalation with gross medical details

jokes.text: What's gonna happen next? I go to my dermatologist, and Johnny's there getting my moles checked?
jokes.setup: Roland's continued paranoid fantasies
jokes.punchline: The specific absurdity of Johnny getting Roland's moles checked
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation. The specificity of mole-checking is grotesquely funny and shows Roland's paranoid spiral.

jokes.text: David's seduction attempt: 'how sexy it is to walk in here and see you doing um... inventory? And wearing those little rubber thimbles'
jokes.setup: David trying to be spontaneous and sexy
jokes.punchline: He finds inventory and office supplies arousing
jokes.explanation: Perfect David character moment - making mundane office work sexual

jokes.text: Patrick's earnest explanation: 'They help me flip the pages easier' followed by David's 'Hot. Hot sex.'
jokes.setup: David's attempt at sexy banter about office supplies
jokes.punchline: Patrick earnestly explains their function while David doubles down
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between Patrick's practicality and David's forced sexiness

jokes.text: Patrick's business response: 'it's kinda hard to build customer loyalty when your store is just like, spontaneously open or closed'
jokes.setup: David suggests spontaneously closing for adventure
jokes.punchline: Patrick points out basic business reality
jokes.explanation: Patrick's logical response undermines David's romantic gestures

jokes.text: David's explanation: 'Alexis's relationship is in trouble... they're in need of a generator, if you know what I mean'
jokes.setup: David needs excuse for afternoon off
jokes.punchline: Uses quiz terminology as if it's actual relationship counseling
jokes.explanation: David treating magazine quiz as serious relationship diagnostic

jokes.text: David's violent fantasy: 'makes me wanna just... rip those little rubber things, and... burn 'em in a fire'
jokes.setup: Patrick's responsible nature
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic destructive impulses toward office supplies
jokes.explanation: David's dramatic overreaction to mundane office supplies is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Stevie's interpretation: 'Fill a big, black empty hole in your life'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Johnny's excessive work since Moira left
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt psychological diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Stevie's characteristic bluntness about emotional issues

jokes.text: If only! At this moment an intestinal bacteria would wreak less havoc on my career!
jokes.setup: Johnny suggesting she drank bad tap water
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic comparison of career problems to intestinal bacteria
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira hyperbole - comparing career concerns to disease with perfect dramatic flair.

jokes.text: I didn't bat an eye when I found out my accommodation here was in actuality a small 'Bed or Breakfast.'
jokes.setup: Moira describing her accommodation issues
jokes.punchline: The malapropism 'Bed or Breakfast' instead of 'Bed and Breakfast'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira malapropism that actually makes logical sense and reveals her priorities.

jokes.text: Moira describes accommodation as 'small Bed or Breakfast. They feed me at work, I'll take the bed.'
jokes.setup: Moira complaining about accommodations
jokes.punchline: Splitting 'Bed and Breakfast' literally and choosing bed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with perfect Moira logic and delivery

jokes.text: Moira's metaphor: 'I refuse to be the goddess on its prow!' about sinking ship
jokes.setup: Director trying to sink the movie
jokes.punchline: Moira sees herself as ship's figurehead in nautical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - dramatic metaphor with self-aggrandizing imagery

jokes.text: That-that was rated 'M' for 'Mature,' John.
jokes.setup: Johnny calling her video game work X-rated
jokes.punchline: Moira's defensive correction about the rating
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Moira's need to defend her professional choices, even video game voice work.

jokes.text: Moira's movie reference: 'If Sandy Bullock hadn't fought to keep the Speed Franchise alive, we never would've had Cruise Control'
jokes.setup: Johnny encouraging her to fight for the movie
jokes.punchline: Moira uses terrible sequel as positive example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira logic - using universally panned sequel as inspiration

jokes.text: Twyla's soup logistics: 'We don't actually have to-go containers for that, so I've just double-bagged it. Would you like a spoon, or a straw?'
jokes.setup: Johnny orders soup to go
jokes.punchline: Twyla offers impossible serving options for bagged soup
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd small-town restaurant logic delivered with straight face

jokes.text: David's height phobia description: 'fearful makes me sound like some Dickensian orphan with a chronic illness. It's more an aversion'
jokes.setup: Alexis mentions David's fear of heights
jokes.punchline: David's pretentious redefinition with literary reference
jokes.explanation: Peak David - refusing simple description in favor of literary pretension

jokes.text: Alexis's descriptions: 'Like a tiny toadstool man, or a little acorn person, or something'
jokes.setup: David wearing safety helmet
jokes.punchline: Alexis's childlike, oddly specific comparisons
jokes.explanation: Alexis's sweet but insulting attempts at description

jokes.text: David's comeback: 'you might wanna talk to half my birthright trip about that'
jokes.setup: Ted calling him a virgin
jokes.punchline: David references sexual experience on Jewish heritage trip
jokes.explanation: Brilliant David comeback combining Jewish identity with sexual bragging

jokes.text: David's excuse for appearance: 'just using a new tinted moisturizer, that's all' when clearly terrified
jokes.setup: Patrick notices David looks flushed/scared
jokes.punchline: David attributes terror symptoms to beauty product
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - vanity as excuse for obvious fear

jokes.text: Moira's behind-scenes complaints: 'None of my phone chargers are working! I ask for a pressed juice, and all I get are blank stares'
jokes.setup: Moira on low-budget movie set
jokes.punchline: Her diva demands are impossibly out of place
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water comedy with Moira's expectations vs reality

jokes.text: Moira's comeback: 'You were the artist, and they were indifferent!' about director's firing
jokes.setup: Director mentions artistic differences led to firing
jokes.punchline: Moira reframes it as studio being 'indifferent' to his art
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay and perfect Moira wit - sophisticated and cutting

jokes.text: They had me play my own father, who then became pregnant despite the vasectomy.
jokes.setup: Moira talking about her soap opera work
jokes.punchline: The increasingly bizarre plot developments
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd soap opera logic - playing own father who gets pregnant post-vasectomy

jokes.text: Moira's soap opera backstory: 'They had me play my own father, who then became pregnant despite the vasectomy'
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her soap opera experience
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd plot involving gender change and impossible pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Absolutely insane soap opera plotting delivered with complete seriousness

jokes.text: Nobody drinks that rocket fuel, Mr. Rose. - I know it tastes like something I found in that gutter out there, but uh... it's the pick-me-up I need
jokes.setup: Stevie commenting on the terrible coffee
jokes.punchline: Johnny acknowledging it tastes like gutter contents but drinking it anyway
jokes.explanation: Decent callback connecting to Johnny's earlier gutter work, showing his desperation and the truly awful coffee.

jokes.text: Stevie's relationship wisdom: 'I hope that someday I find someone who I can stand long enough to feel a little lost when they're not around'
jokes.setup: Johnny missing Moira
jokes.punchline: Stevie's cynical but sweet definition of love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - cynical but ultimately romantic, beautifully written

jokes.text: Eight days, Stevie. That's the longest we've gone without seeing each other.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining why he's struggling
jokes.punchline: The surprisingly romantic admission about his marriage
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that reframes Johnny's behavior as romantic devotion rather than dependency. Good emotional grounding.

jokes.text: Alexis and Patrick's rope course puns: 'I really hope we didn't rope you into something here'
jokes.setup: David struggling on rope course
jokes.punchline: Obvious rope/rope pun while he's in distress
jokes.explanation: Weak obvious pun that doesn't land well in the moment

jokes.text: He and Anderson Cooper were stuck up there for like, three hours, until the wind died down.
jokes.setup: Explaining David's fear of heights
jokes.punchline: The casual Anderson Cooper reference in David's backstory
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing David's former glamorous life. Anderson Cooper is a perfect random celebrity for this story.

jokes.text: Alexis's confession: 'I took the quiz like, 15 times. In Need of a Generator is like, the only answer you can get'
jokes.setup: David's relationship insecurity based on quiz
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits the quiz was rigged all along
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes earlier story but expected

jokes.text: 'In Need of a Generator' is like, the only answer you can get.
jokes.setup: David taking the relationship quiz seriously
jokes.punchline: Alexis revealing the quiz is rigged to give bad results
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that reframes the earlier quiz anxiety. Shows how David got worked up over nothing.

jokes.text: David's love confession: 'I love when you use words like inventory! I even love those stupid rubber things you put on your fingers, 'cause you think they flip the pages faster. They don't flip the pages faster!'
jokes.setup: David panicking about their relationship while hanging in tree
jokes.punchline: Declares love for mundane things, then reveals harsh truth about rubber thimbles
jokes.explanation: Perfect David emotional moment - romantic gesture undermined by practical truth. Brilliant character writing.

jokes.text: Moira's final crow speech: 'Listen to me... aaawk! The day will come when we are no longer social outcasts! I am but a tailfeather away from finding the cure. So please... quiet your caws so that we may take up our cause: redemption!'
jokes.setup: Moira fully committed to terrible crow movie
jokes.punchline: Delivers passionate speech with crow mannerisms and bird puns
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira performance - total commitment to ridiculous material with perfect delivery

jokes.text: In terms of my eyeline, how many birds am I clocking? And of them, how many are mutants?
jokes.setup: Director mentioning digital birds in the scene
jokes.punchline: Moira's detailed technical questions about imaginary birds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira professionalism - treating a ridiculous B-movie with serious actor preparation and specific technical questions.
